Investor Anatomy
Global Growth and Economic Cycle
Home
Global Growth and Economic Cycle
Aggregates commodity positioning, cycle momentum proxies, and metals-led demand signals to assess the current global growth and economic cycle regime.

Regime Assessment:

  • Transitional / Fragile Balance
  • Regime Confidence Index: Medium Confidence
  • The regime is fragile due to high positioning tension across major commodity benchmarks.

Why This Regime:

  • The primary driver is a strong cyclical demand tailwind in industrial metals, where copper is significantly "rich" relative to energy and FX controls (1).
  • Dynamic weighting prioritizes this high-weight cyclical tailwind (1) over mixed tactical signals.
  • Tactical de-risking and speculative unwinding in Gold (2), Copper (3), and Crude Oil (4) constrain the regime to a transitional state.
  • Downweighted signals include Platinum (5), Palladium (6), and Copper CoT (3) due to low conviction bands and interpretation confidence.

Alignment & Tensions:

  • Cyclical demand for metals (1) reinforces pro-cyclical flow momentum in rates-related economic proxies (7).
  • Tensions exist where extreme speculative crowding in Lithium (8) and high tension in Gold (2) oppose the structural expansion narrative.
  • These tensions do not overturn the regime because the underlying industrial demand residual remains at an extreme positive z-score (1).

Scenario Balance:

  • Dominant scenario: Tactical consolidation as "stale longs" liquidate while industrial demand remains robust.
  • Primary upside risk: A short-covering squeeze in Lithium or Silver triggered by positive newsflow (8)(9).
  • Primary downside risk: A sharp correction if positioning tension in Gold or Oil leads to an uncontrolled liquidation cascade (2)(4).

What Would Change the Regime:

  • A decline in the Metals_Tailwind z-score to ≤ 0.75, indicating a collapse in copper-specific demand (1).
  • A shift in 4-week speculative flows from negative to positive in major benchmarks, signaling renewed sponsorship (2)(3).
Inputs
GOLD - Individual Market Analysis

Signal Summary:

  • Configuration statement: Given a speculative net position of 23.56% (1), a Hedger_zscore of 2.81, and a negative 4-week flow of -2.47, this setup aligns with Transition-biased price paths and Normal volatility, where the dominant risk is Long liquidation, not a short squeeze.
  • The signal currently reflects a "Spec_Long_Unwind" regime following a peak in positioning tension, as speculative sponsorship fades despite a historically less-short commercial stance (1).
  • Conviction Band: Medium; Interpretation Confidence: High Confidence; Internal Conflict Flag: No.

Methodology Applied:

  • Hybrid Asset Priority: Weights flow turns (4-week flow) and macro validation over absolute positioning levels for gold (1).
  • Structural Baseline Rule: Commercial net short positions are treated as "normal"; historical stretch (z-score) is used to identify imbalance (1).
  • Fragility Overlay: High positioning tension (>3.0) and extreme Hedger z-scores (>2.0) signal market imbalance and mean-reversion pressure (1).
  • Latest observation date: 2026-02-17.

Key Dynamics:

  • The primary driver is the ongoing reduction in speculative net longs (Spec_Long_Unwind) paired with price/positioning divergence (1).
  • Positioning tension remains high at 3.86, suggesting that while flows have turned negative, the market structure remains fragile from recent crowding (1).
  • Stabilisation is not yet evident, as 4-week flow momentum continues to penalise the long side.
  • Conditional Invalidation: A pivot in 4-week speculative flow back to positive "Long Build" alongside a breakout in price momentum.

Scenario Balance:

  • Base Case dominant: Tactical consolidation or moderate downside as speculative "stale longs" continue to exit.
  • Upside secondary: Macro-driven re-leveraging triggered by a sharp decline in real yields or US dollar weakness.
  • Downside residual: Accelerating long liquidation if prices breach technical support levels while positioning tension remains high.

Time Horizon & Aggregation:

  • Tactical (weeks); guided by high-frequency flow shifts and near-term long-unwinding dynamics.
  • Aggregation Weight Hint: Medium; positioning is stretched but requires macro-signal alignment for a higher-conviction structural call.

Macro Relevance:

  • Informs global sentiment and liquidity dimensions, specifically the risk-appetite for non-yielding macro hedges.
  • Cycle position: Late-stage tactical expansion currently undergoing a momentum correction.
  • Interacts typically with real interest rate trends and US dollar regime signals to validate the persistence of positioning extremes.

Data & References:

  • Latest observation: 2026-02-17 (GOLD - COMMODITY EXCHANGE INC.) (1).
  • Most influential datapoints: Hedger_zscore (2.81) and Flow_4w (-2.47) (1).
  • Additional references: US Real Interest Rate Trend; US Dollar Index (DXY) momentum.
SILVER - Individual Market Analysis

Signal Summary:

  • Configuration statement: Given a Spec_zscore of -1.95 and Hedger_zscore of 1.83, this setup aligns with Upward-biased price paths and Elevated volatility, where the dominant risk is Mean reversion, not Trend continuation. (1)
  • The signal is classified as "Balanced Long Bias" with high internal tension as positioning approaches a speculative extreme short stretch. (2)
  • Conviction Band: Medium; Interpretation Confidence: High Confidence; Internal Conflict Flag: No.

Methodology Applied:

  • Positioning stretch is measured by z-scores; values between 1 and 2 are classified as "Stretched" (Spec_zscore -1.95). (1)
  • Hedger extremes (1.83) are interpreted as mean-reversion pressure rather than momentum signals. (1)
  • Silver-specific priors elevate volatility expectations and emphasize short-covering convexity when speculative stretch is near extremes. (2)
  • Dataset: SILVER - COMMODITY EXCHANGE INC. (latest observation: 2026-02-17). (1)

Key Dynamics:

  • Main drivers include the contraction of speculative length (z-score -1.95) and hedgers moving to "Deep Long" status. (1)
  • Negative 4-week flow (-2.00) indicates active long reduction, suggesting the recent price decline is losing sponsorship. (1)
  • High positioning tension is emerging as speculators are significantly less long than the 52-week average. (1)
  • Conditional Invalidation: A Spec_zscore drop below -2.0 would trigger a "Crowded Short" regime, increasing downside air-pocket risk. (2)

Scenario Balance:

  • Base case dominant: Mean reversion and price stabilization as speculative selling reaches historical exhaustion levels.
  • Upside secondary: A sharp short-covering squeeze if 4-week flows flip positive amid high positioning tension.
  • Downside residual: Liquidation cascade if price breaks technical supports, forcing a transition to an extreme crowding regime.

Time Horizon & Aggregation:

  • Time Horizon: Tactical (weeks); CoT signals are optimized for identifying near-term reversal quality and fragility. (1)
  • Aggregation Weight Hint: Medium; the stretch is significant but has not yet reached the "Extreme" |z| ≥ 2 threshold.

Macro Relevance:

  • Informs the pricing and sentiment dimensions by identifying when market participants are positioned for a reversal.
  • Reflects a late-correction cycle position where speculative washout typically precedes a period of stabilization.
  • Interacts with USD and real-rate signals to confirm macro sponsorship of price turns. (2)

Data & References:

  • SILVER - COMMODITY EXCHANGE INC. (2026-02-17). (1)
  • Spec_zscore (-1.95) and Hedger_zscore (1.83) are the primary directional drivers.
  • Inclusion of the USD Index Signal and Real Interest Rate Trend Signal would improve reliability.
Platinum - Individual Market Analysis

Signal Summary:

  • Configuration statement: Given a neutral Spec Net %OI of 4.94%, a Spec z-score of -0.567, and persistent negative flow, this setup aligns with Downward-biased price paths and Normal volatility, where the dominant risk is Long liquidation, not a short squeeze (1).
  • The signal is currently in a Balanced regime with a "Hedger Adding Short" market structure, indicating a lack of speculative sponsorship and active commercial hedging (1).
  • Conviction Band: Low; Interpretation Confidence: Low Confidence; Internal Conflict Flag: No.

Methodology Applied:

  • Stretch Rules: Interprets Spec z-scores where |z| < 1 is classified as "normal/un-stretched" (applied to current z-score of -0.567) (1).
  • Flow Rules: Defines flow opposing net direction (Negative Flow vs. Positive Net Position) as conviction fading or de-risking (1).
  • Confidence Grading: Assigns "Low" confidence when the market shows a directional net position without stretch or a fresh flow turn (1).
  • Dataset: PLATINUM - NYMEX Commitment of Traders (CoT) Disaggregated Report, latest observation February 17, 2026 (1).

Key Dynamics:

  • Speculative de-risking is the primary driver, as evidenced by four consecutive weeks of negative 4-week flow (-2.01 current) (1).
  • Price is exhibiting downward momentum, falling from 237.78 to 182.93, while speculative net contracts have dwindled to 3,426 (1).
  • Hedgers are adding to short positions (Net contracts -12,659), reinforcing the bearish market structure (1).
  • Conditional Invalidation: A move in speculative z-score above |1.0| or a shift to positive 4-week flow indicating renewed long conviction (1).

Scenario Balance:

  • Base case dominant: Continued price softening as long liquidation persists in an un-stretched market.
  • Upside risk: Tactical bounce triggered by a shift from the industrial regime to a precious-metal substitution narrative.
  • Downside risk: Acceleration of selling if industrial demand signals (auto-catalyst sector) deteriorate further.

Time Horizon & Aggregation:

  • Time Horizon: Tactical (weeks), based on the responsiveness of 4-week flow and CoT reporting frequency.
  • Aggregation Weight Hint: Low, due to the lack of extreme positioning stretch and low conviction grading (1).

Macro Relevance:

  • Informs global demand and industrial pricing dimensions, particularly within the automotive sector.
  • Cycle position: Late-cycle or slowdown, as indicated by speculative exit and falling prices.
  • Typically interacts with Gold and Palladium signals to determine if "precious" or "industrial" narratives are dominant.

Data & References:

  • CoT Disaggregated Futures: Platinum (NYMEX), observation date February 17, 2026 (1).
  • Most influential: Negative Flow_4w and the decline in Spec_Net_%OI to 4.94%.
  • Additional reliability: Global auto-catalyst demand data and Palladium CoT positioning for substitution context.
Palladium - Individual Market Analysis

Signal Summary:

  • Configuration statement (mandatory): Given a Spec Net %OI of 3.17%, a Spec Z-score of 1.27, and positive 4-week flow of 4.51, this setup aligns with Upward-biased price paths and Normal volatility, where the dominant risk is Trend continuation, not Mean reversion. (1)
  • Positioning is currently classified as stretched to the long side with conviction building as flow aligns with direction. (1)
  • Conviction Band: Low; Interpretation Confidence: Low Confidence; Internal Conflict Flag: No.

Methodology Applied:

  • Stretch Rule: Spec Z-score between 1 and 2 is classified as "stretched" rather than "extreme." (1)
  • Flow Rule: 4-week flow (+4.51) aligned with net direction indicates "conviction building." (1)
  • Positioning Tension: Measured by the reversal score, which currently flags no clear tactical reversal setup. (1)
  • Data timeliness cannot be assessed (latest observation provided: 2026-02-17).

Key Dynamics:

  • Main drivers include positive speculative flow momentum and a Spec Z-score of 1.27 indicating moderate stretch. (1)
  • Flow is currently reinforcing the net directional move, suggesting sponsorship of the recent price trend. (1)
  • Internal tensions are low, with hedgers in a "covering" state rather than aggressive supply expansion. (1)
  • Conditional Invalidation: A shift to negative 4-week flow (long reduction) would indicate conviction fading. (1)

Scenario Balance:

  • Base Case dominant: Continued upward drift supported by aligned speculative flows.
  • Upside risk: Non-linear price response if thin liquidity interacts with further long building.
  • Downside risk: Sudden long liquidation if 4-week flow momentum turns negative.

Time Horizon & Aggregation:

  • Time Horizon: Tactical (weeks), driven by the weight of 4-week flow dynamics. (1)
  • Aggregation Weight Hint: Low, necessitated by the inability to confirm data freshness and the inherent volatility of palladium.

Macro Relevance:

  • Macro dimension informed: Pricing and Sentiment.
  • Cycle position: Mid-cycle trend reinforcement.
  • Typically interacts with USD regime signals and global industrial production trends.

Data & References:

  • CFTC Palladium Disaggregated Report (latest: 2026-02-17). (1)
  • Influential datapoints: Spec Z-score (1.27) and 4-week Flow (+4.51). (1)
  • Secondary indicators: Open Interest stability and South African production news.
Copper - Individual Market Analysis

Signal Summary:

  • Configuration statement: Given a Spec_Net_%OI of 19.7%, a Spec_zscore of 0.40, and negative Flow_4w of -2.86, this setup aligns with Downward-biased price paths and Normal volatility, where the dominant risk is Mean reversion as flow opposes net direction per Rule 11.5 (1).
  • The signal is currently in a Bearish Reversal Risk regime, characterized by a High_Tension_Low_Flags synthetic state as price sponsorship fades (1).
  • Conviction Band: Low; Interpretation Confidence: Low Confidence; Internal Conflict Flag: No.

Methodology Applied:

  • Stretch Rule: Spec z-scores between -1 and +1, such as the current 0.40, are classified as "Normal/Un-stretched" (Rule 11.4) (1).
  • Flow Rule: The negative 4-week flow (-2.86) opposing the net long position indicates conviction is fading and early turn risk is rising (Rule 11.5) (1).
  • Copper Supplement: Hedger net short positions are normal, but the -1.68 z-score suggests moderate mean-reversion pressure (Section 4.1) (1).
  • Data Source: Copper Individual Market Analysis; Latest observation: 2026-02-17 (1).

Key Dynamics:

  • The primary driver is the "Long Reduction" flow (-2.86), which marks a loss of speculative sponsorship for the previous rally (1).
  • Momentum is currently negative, with price declining from ~53.00 to 48.80 alongside falling speculative commitment (1).
  • Internal tension exists between the residual net long position and the accelerating exit of speculative capital.
  • Conditional Invalidation: Spec_zscore exceeding +2.0 (Crowded Long regime).

Scenario Balance:

  • Base case dominant: Tactical price drift lower as long positions continue to liquidate in a non-stretched environment.
  • Upside secondary: Short-covering bounce triggered by a sudden weakening of the USD regime.
  • Downside residual: Sharp correction if industrial growth data confirms a cyclical peak, forcing rapid hedger adjustment.

Time Horizon & Aggregation:

  • Time Horizon: Tactical (weeks) as the signal responds to 4-week flow turns and weekly CoT shifts.
  • Aggregation Weight Hint: Low, due to the lack of extreme positioning stretch and low conviction grading.

Macro Relevance:

  • Informs global demand and pricing dimensions through the lens of industrial metal speculative appetite (2).
  • Cycle position: Suggests a potential mid-cycle slowdown or consolidation phase as sponsorship wanes.
  • Typically interacts with USD strength signals and China-sensitive risk sentiment indicators.

Data & References:

  • Copper - Individual Market Analysis: 2026-02-17 (1).
  • Datapoints: Spec_zscore (0.40), Flow_4w (-2.86), Hedger_zscore (-1.68), Price (48.80).
  • Public datasets for depth: LME Copper Warehouse Stocks, China Caixin Manufacturing PMI.
Lithium Hydroxide - Individual Market Analysis

Signal Summary:

  • Configuration statement: Given a Spec Net %OI of -42.2% and a Speculative z-score of -2.85 (1), this setup aligns with Transition-biased price paths and Elevated volatility, where the dominant risk is Short covering / squeeze, not Trend continuation.
  • The market is in a Crowded Short regime (1) characterized by extreme speculative shorting and significant hedger net long positioning.
  • Conviction Band: Medium; Interpretation Confidence: Mixed Signals; Internal Conflict Flag: No.

Methodology Applied:

  • Speculative z-scores reaching |z| ≥ 2 signify a Crowding-Risk regime (1), indicating high fragility.
  • A Crowded Short label is triggered when Spec_zscore ≤ −2, suggesting upside convexity and elevated squeeze potential (1).
  • Lithium-specific rules mandate downgrading confidence due to evolving market depth and potential baseline instability (1).
  • Data Freshness: Stale. The latest observation is dated 2026-02-17 (1).

Key Dynamics:

  • Speculative net positioning is at extreme 1-year lows (z-score -2.85), creating a "fragility up" environment (1).
  • Four-week flow is aggressively negative (-9.4%), showing high conviction in the short trend despite extreme stretch (1).
  • Hedgers are at a Deep Long extreme (z-score 2.46), providing mean-reversion pressure against speculative shorts (1).
  • Conditional Invalidation: Speculative flow turning positive (short covering) or price stabilization would confirm a squeeze setup (1).

Scenario Balance:

  • Dominant base case: Trend exhaustion as speculative crowding reaches non-linear fragility limits.
  • Most plausible upside risk: A sharp short squeeze triggered by positive newsflow or short covering.
  • Most plausible downside risk: Continued price discovery to the downside if liquidity allows for further expansion.

Time Horizon & Aggregation:

  • Time Horizon: Tactical (weeks) as CoT signals primarily inform near-term reversal and fragility risks (1).
  • Aggregation Weight Hint: Medium; lithium's emerging status requires caution despite high statistical stretch (1).

Macro Relevance:

  • Informs global demand and trade sentiment regarding the EV supply chain (1).
  • Cycle position: Late-stage price discovery or trend exhaustion.
  • Typically interacts with USD regime and industrial production signals to confirm global growth momentum.

Data & References:

  • Lithium Hydroxide CoT Diagnostics (2026-02-17) (1).
  • Speculative z-score and Crowded Short flag are the most influential metrics.
  • Additional reliability would be gained from Lithium Carbonate spot prices and EV production data.
Crude Oil - Individual Market Analysis

Signal Summary:

  • Configuration statement: Given a Spec_zscore of 1.61, a Hedger_zscore of -1.37, and Flat flow (0.00), this setup aligns with Transition-biased price paths and Normal volatility, where the dominant risk is Mean reversion, not Trend continuation. (1)
  • Current state is classified as High Tension with Low Flags, indicating a stretched but non-extreme market structure. (1)
  • Conviction Band: Low; Interpretation Confidence: Mixed Signals; Internal Conflict Flag: Yes.

Methodology Applied:

  • Stretch threshold: |z| ≥ 1 identifies a stretched market, while |z| ≥ 2 marks extreme crowding. (1)
  • Flow Rule: Flow of 0.00 against stretched z-scores indicates conviction fading or sponsorship exhaustion. (1)
  • WTI Physical Gate: High-confidence calls require validation from inventory direction and curve shape (backwardation/contango). (1)
  • Latest observation date: 2026-02-17.

Key Dynamics:

  • Main drivers are the stretched speculative z-score (1.61) and hedger z-score (-1.37), flagging rising fragility. (1)
  • Stalled momentum is evident as 4-week flow reached 0.00, suggesting price stabilisation near $75.73. (1)
  • Internal tension exists due to active divergence between speculative and commercial participants. (1)
  • Conditional Invalidation: Emergence of strong positive 4-week flow (>0.5) or physical inventory draw confirmation. (1)

Scenario Balance:

  • Dominant base case: Mean reversion or range-bound activity as stretched positions lack fresh flow sponsorship.
  • Upside risk: Squeeze-biased volatility if sudden supply shocks trigger short-covering in a deep-short hedger regime.
  • Downside risk: Liquidation risk if physical indicators (inventories) deteriorate while long-side stretch persists.

Time Horizon & Aggregation:

  • Time Horizon: Tactical (weeks) reflecting the CoT reporting lag and flow sensitivity.
  • Aggregation Weight Hint: Medium, justified by the presence of significant positioning stretch and internal divergence.

Macro Relevance:

  • Informs the pricing and sentiment dimensions of the energy complex and global risk tone.
  • Positioned mid-to-late cycle where positioning extremes typically precede volatility expansion.
  • Interacts with USD strength and inflation breakevens to gauge commodity-led cost-push pressures.

Data & References:

  • WTI Financial Crude Oil - NYMEX CoT (2026-02-17). (1)
  • Spec_Net_%OI (0.0) and Positioning Tension Score (2.98) were most influential. (1)
  • EIA Weekly Petroleum Status Report and WTI Term Structure (1M-6M spreads) would improve reliability.
Forward Indicators of Economic Cycles (Momentum of Flows)

Signal Summary:

  • Given positive avg_flow_4w (1.08) and a dominant trend_confirm_share (0.36) (1), this setup aligns with Upward-biased price paths and Normal volatility, where the dominant risk is Trend continuation, not Regime shift.
  • The Rates sector remains in a pro-cyclical flow regime, though 1-week momentum is softening.
  • Conviction Band: Medium; Interpretation Confidence: High Confidence; Internal Conflict Flag: No.

Methodology Applied:

  • Trend-confirming flows (trend_confirm_share) reinforce existing crowded positions to signal pro-cyclical regimes (1).
  • Flow momentum (avg_flow_4w_mom) improvement signals acceleration, while negative values warn of potential slowdowns.
  • Contrarian flows (contrarian_flow_share) identify risks of reversal when they oppose existing crowding.
  • Forward Indicators of Economic Cycles (Momentum of Flows) (1); latest observation: 2026-02-17.

Key Dynamics:

  • Net speculative flows are positive (1.08), supported by a positive flow share of 0.54 (1).
  • Momentum has turned negative (-0.21), suggesting the current flow expansion phase is losing its immediate acceleration.
  • Internal coherence is high as trend-confirming flows (0.36) significantly outweigh contrarian flows (0.09).
  • Conditional Invalidation: A spike in contrarian_flow_share above 0.20 or a flip to negative avg_flow_4w.

Scenario Balance:

  • Base case dominant: Continued pro-cyclical flow support for current rate trends given low contrarian activity.
  • Most plausible upside risk: Re-acceleration of flow momentum triggered by renewed macro trend conviction.
  • Most plausible downside risk: Slowdown or topping pattern if negative momentum persists and contrarian flows rise.

Time Horizon & Aggregation:

  • Tactical (weeks); grounded in the 4-week rolling speculative flow window (1).
  • Aggregation Weight Hint: Medium; represents a high-confidence tactical confirmation of existing sector trends.

Macro Relevance:

  • Informs the Liquidity and Demand dimensions of the macro cycle.
  • Cycle position: Mature expansion (pro-cyclical flows with fading momentum).
  • Typically confirms or leads direction in the US Treasury Yield Curve Signal (2).

Data & References:

  • Forward Indicators of Economic Cycles (Momentum of Flows) (1); date: 2026-02-17.
  • Key metrics: avg_flow_4w (1.08), avg_flow_4w_mom (-0.21), and trend_confirm_share (0.36).
  • SOF 3M - Individual Market Analysis (3) and US Treasury 10-Year Notes (4) would improve cross-tenor reliability.
Energy-Adjusted Metals Tailwind Signal

Signal Summary:

  • Configuration statement (mandatory): Given a Copper price of 5.79, WTI Oil at 62.53, and a Metals_Tailwind z-score of 2.72, this setup aligns with Upward-biased price paths and Elevated volatility, where the dominant risk is Trend continuation, not Mean reversion.
  • The signal is in a Demand_Tailwind regime, indicating copper is significantly "rich" relative to energy and FX controls (1).
  • Conviction Band: Extreme; Interpretation Confidence: High Confidence; Internal Conflict Flag: No.

Methodology Applied:

  • Regresses Copper levels on WTI Oil (energy input) and the Broad USD Index (FX headwind) to isolate idiosyncratic demand (1).
  • Maps robust z-scores of the Copper_Residual to regimes: Demand_Tailwind (>0.75), Neutral, or Headwind (<-0.75) (1).
  • Latest observation date: 2026-02-28 (1).

Key Dynamics:

  • Primary driver is the extreme divergence (z = 2.72) between realized copper prices and fitted macro expectations (1).
  • Strong demand-side momentum has persisted since late 2025, suggesting structural inventory tightness or industrial expansion (1).
  • No internal tensions; copper prices, residuals, and regime classifications are positively aligned.
  • Conditional Invalidation: A decline in the Metals_Tailwind z-score to ≤ 0.75 (1).

Scenario Balance:

  • Base case dominant: Sustained industrial demand tailwinds maintain copper’s price premium.
  • Upside secondary: Supply-side disruptions or inventory depletion triggers a price squeeze.
  • Downside residual: Sharp global manufacturing slowdown collapses the copper-specific demand premium.

Time Horizon & Aggregation:

  • Time Horizon: Cyclical (months) as the signal filters for multi-month demand trends over noise.
  • Aggregation Weight Hint: High; copper is a primary leading indicator for global industrial cycles (2).

Macro Relevance:

  • Informs the global demand and industrial pricing dimensions (3).
  • Cycle position: Mid-to-late expansion, characterized by strong demand and rising commodity residuals.
  • Typically confirms risk-on positioning breadth across other industrial metals and energy sectors.

Data & References:

  • COMEX Copper (HG=F), WTI Crude (DCOILWTICO), and Broad USD Index (DTWEXBGS) (1).
  • Copper_Residual z-score (2.72) is the most influential datapoint.
  • LME Warehouse Levels and Global Manufacturing PMIs would improve demand reliability.
Investor Anatomy - Global Growth and Economic Cycle - Not Investment Advice
The information presented is for general informational purposes only and does not constitute financial or investment advice. It has been prepared without regard to individual objectives, financial situation, or needs. You should consider whether it is appropriate for your circumstances and seek independent advice where necessary.
